purpose: Rigorous disproof of the congruent refinement S_6 -> S_12 via the edge-length multiset congruence invariant.
paper_reference: Theorem 4.2 (Disproof of S_6 -> S_12), Congruent Dissections of the Regular Tetrahedron

embedding.note: Regular tetrahedron with |AB| = 1. Squared distances use |P-Q|^2 = dx^2 + 3*dy_r^2 + 6*dz_r^2 with y_r = y/sqrt(3), z_r = z/sqrt(6) (exact rational arithmetic).

invariant_used.name: Squared-edge multiset (a congruence invariant)
invariant_used.key_observation: The minimum of the squared-edge multiset is a congruence invariant. min(S12) = 1/24.

enumeration.lemma: A plane that cuts a tetrahedron into two tetrahedra must contain exactly one edge of the tetrahedron and meet the opposite edge at a single interior point. For the two halves to be congruent (equal volume 1/2), the cut point on the opposite edge must be its midpoint.
enumeration.consequence: For S6 there are exactly 3 opposite-edge pairs; each yields 2 halves (tetrahedra). Total: 6 candidate half-tetrahedra, exhaustively enumerated below.

conclusion.result: No congruent-halving cut of S6 produces a piece congruent to S12. Q.E.D.

arithmetic: All computations performed in exact rational arithmetic (Python Fractions). No floating-point approximations.
